facts.title: Foundation of the Duomo
facts.body: Construction of the Duomo di Milano began in 1386 under Archbishop Antonio da Saluzzo and continued for nearly six centuries, a span that marked Milan's transition from medieval commune to modern metropolis.

facts.title: Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II
facts.body: Designed by Giuseppe Mengoni and completed between 1865 and 1877, the Galleria is one of the world's oldest shopping arcades and forms the northern edge of Piazza del Duomo.

facts.title: Piazza Duomo during WWII
facts.body: In August 1943 Allied bombing severely damaged buildings around Piazza Duomo. The cathedral itself was struck but its main structure survived, and the square became a symbolic stage for Milan's 1945 liberation.
